what_it_does: Defines a TopBar JSX component that renders a notification bell with an optional count badge and a user avatar, then exposes the component on window.TopBar.
observations: TopBar accepts a notifications prop with a default value of 7.
The notification badge renders only when notifications is greater than 0.
Both the bell and avatar are inline SVGs inside span elements.
The bell span has role="button" and aria-label="Notifications".
The component is attached to the global window object instead of being exported.
standouts: Compact topbar component focused only on notification and avatar affordances.
Uses file-local inline SVG paths rather than external icon dependencies.
Global window.TopBar assignment suggests script-style consumption.
conventions: Uses cv-* class names for styling hooks.
Uses conditional rendering with && for the badge.
Does not use ES module import/export syntax.
concerns: The bell uses a span with role="button" but no tabIndex or keyboard handler is present in this file.
The inline SVGs do not specify aria-hidden, title, or focus handling in this file.
The visual pinning described in the comment depends on CSS not shown in this file.
notes: Small platform topbar widget providing notification and avatar controls through global registration.
questions: Where are the cv-topbar, cv-bell, cv-bell-badge, and cv-avatar styles defined?
Is the notification bell intended to be interactive beyond its role and aria label?
Should the component be consumed globally through window.TopBar or through a module export?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose mostly matches the implementation: it renders a bell and avatar, while the pinned top-right behavior is not enforced in this file itself.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to provide a lightweight reusable top-right account and notification control for a ConceptV platform UI without depending on imported assets.
novelty: The distinct feature is the combination of self-contained JSX icons with a global window.TopBar registration in a small UI kit component.
contribution: Contributes a compact topbar substrate containing notification count display, avatar affordance, styling hooks, and a globally reachable component entry point for integration into a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: TopBar.jsx defines a JSX topbar component with a notification bell, conditional badge, avatar icon, cv-* class hooks, and global window.TopBar exposure.
intention_for_embedding: This was for adding a small platform header control area with notifications and user identity affordance to a ConceptV design system UI kit.

symbols.does: Renders a topbar containing a notification bell, conditional badge, and avatar icon.
symbols.description: TopBar is a JSX functional component with a default notifications count of 7. It creates a cv-topbar wrapper, shows a bell control with an aria label, conditionally displays the numeric badge when the count is positive, and includes an avatar SVG. The component is made available globally through window.TopBar.
